key_points: Law refers to the system of rules created by the government to regulate behavior and maintain order in society
Social justice means ensuring fairness, equality and protection of rights for all individuals
Laws are created to protect workers from exploitation and unsafe working conditions
The government sets rules such as minimum wage and safety standards for industries
Companies must follow labour laws to ensure fair treatment and protection of workers
Courts and government authorities enforce laws to maintain justice and equality
examples: Minimum wage laws require employers to pay workers a fixed minimum salary
Factory laws ensure safe working environments and limit working hours
Child labour laws prevent children from being employed in hazardous work
Courts can punish companies that violate labour and safety laws
formulas: Social justice → Equality + Protection of rights
Worker protection → Labour laws + Safety regulations
exam_tips: Explain the relationship between law and social justice clearly
Mention examples such as minimum wage and safety laws
Describe how laws protect workers from exploitation
Use key terms like labour laws, equality and worker rights in answers
